mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns exceed zero in >60% of instances where UNRATE surpasses 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline condition yields positive forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and corporate output, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during recovery phases, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation (Rank IC < 0.01) with INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with accelerating inflation compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by >0.25% while CPIAUCSL rises >0.4% month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered periods, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment drop exceeding 100,000 workers signals severe labor market contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 100,000 in a single month
failure_condition: The threshold crossing fails to predict negative returns, with positive outcomes occurring in >60% of instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects robust aggregate economic activity, which directly supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns are negative in >50% of high-GDP observations.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate drop below 2.0 percent indicates aggressive monetary accommodation that rapidly boosts equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of instances when the rate crosses below 2.0%, or the signal loses predictive accuracy (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample validation.
